reasoning: The market was priced at 3% on November 13, 2025. Since then, careful checks of recent news covering major hotspots (Syria, Ukraine/Russia borders, global airspace intercepts) have shown no evidence of any direct military encounter or use of weaponry between US and Russian forces. The resolution criteria are very strict, expressly excluding non-violent actions (airspace violations, cyberattacks), warning shots, and even intentional ramming/physical collisions without the direct use of weapons (such as the 2023 drone incident). Such restrictions mean that only an intentional or severe accidental kinetic shootout would qualify. Because both militaries operate under robust de-confliction protocols specifically designed to avoid a direct war between nuclear-armed nations, the baseline probability for such an event is near zero. With only about 38 days remaining until the December 31, 2025 resolution date, and no signs of escalating direct conflict, the probability has slightly decayed from the mid-November initial Polymarket baseline. Thus, a 2% estimate accurately reflects the remaining time frame and strict resolution conditions.
